results.snippet: Legislation is published in a two tier arrangement: Acts of Parliament (otherwise known as "statutes" or "primary legislation"), and Statutory Instruments (otherwise known as "secondary legislation" or "delegated legislation"). Acts of Parliament. Acts are characterized either as Public General Acts or ...

results.snippet: In Great Britain, proposed legislation first appears as a Bill before Parliament, where it is debated. A Bill is a draft law; it becomes an Act if it is approved by a majority in the House of Commons and House of Lords, and formally agreed to by the reigning monarch (known as Royal Assent).
